highlights.text: I provided this setup way back on F riday June 20th at 4pm, when we were 6020, by stating: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend, until it ends.
My lean is we work back up to 6050, then 6081.
I wrote last Monday June 30th (when we were 6250) that: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend until it ends.
My general lean is that 6300 to 6250 may be a new consolidation range with 6281 a key pivot inside.” We spent **all day today** pinned around 6281, as we coiled tight.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the last few days action where we (very rarely) saw all three of my setup types: We saw a Failed Breakdown last Wednesday at 6240 which started the terminal leg to 6330+, we saw a Back-Test Long on Friday at 6281, and we saw yesterday the rarest of all my setup types: The Breakdown Short, and this was the first in many weeks.
5978-82 to 6081= a range (descending triangle) with 6050 a key magnet inside.

post_title: After 10 Green Days In A Row, SPX Is Coiled Tight. Move Incoming, What Way? July 9 Plan
